rulings.Case Title: ELENA ASTURIAS ET AL VS. NEWREZ, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 1, 2022 line 1. Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. Plaintiffs' moving papers fail to show a probability of prevailing on the merits and imminent harm. Plaintiffs no longer seek to engage in the loan modification process, to the extent that Defendant has not remedied their violations. Plaintiffs also fail to establish that injury is imminent. The NOTS has expired and must be re-recorded. No sale date for the property is currently set. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ELENA ASTURIAS ET AL VS. NEWREZ, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 1, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT NEWREZ LLC, F/K/A NEW PENN FINANCIAL, LLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Plaintiff has 30 days to file and serve an amended complaint. Demurrer to the cause of action for violation of CC 2923.7 is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts demonstrating why the SPOC's alleged failure to fulfill their duties under the statute between March 2020 and August 2020 was material. Demurrer to the cause of action for violation of CC 2924.10 is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts demonstrating why Defendant's failure to provide Ps with the expiration dates of documents was material. Demurrer to cause of action for violation of UCL is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts establishing standing. Demurrer to causes of action for violation CC 2923.6 and negligence are overruled. These causes of action are sufficiently alleged. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES Z. CHOU VS. SHUZHEN HUANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 1, 2022 line 10. DEFENDANT SHUZHEN HUANG, LISHENG ZHOU, STEPHANIE ZHOU DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege that the license was revoked lawfully in compliance with CCP section 1161(1). The complaint includes sufficient specificity on the identity of the Plaintiff. Defendant cites no authority that UD action based on revocation of license requires an affirmative pleading of exemption from the rent ordinance. Plaintiff has 5 days to file and serve an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
